children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: a Grants authorized
children.text: The Attorney General may award block grants to an eligible entity to develop, improve, or expand domestic child human trafficking deterrence programs that assist law enforcement officers, prosecutors, judicial officials, and qualified victims’ services organizations in collaborating to rescue and restore the lives of victims, while investigating and prosecuting offenses involving child human trafficking.

children.label: A
children.text: identify victims and acts of child human trafficking;

children.label: B
children.text: address the unique needs of child victims of human trafficking;

children.label: C
children.text: facilitate the rescue of child victims of human trafficking;

children.label: D
children.text: investigate and prosecute acts of human trafficking, including the soliciting, patronizing, or purchasing of commercial sex acts from children, as well as training to build cases against complex criminal networks involved in child human trafficking; and

children.label: E
children.text: utilize, implement, and provide education on safe harbor laws enacted by States, aimed at preventing the criminalization and prosecution of child sex trafficking victims for prostitution offenses, and other laws aimed at the investigation and prosecution of child human trafficking;

children.label: A
children.text: funding salaries, in whole or in part, for law enforcement officers, including patrol officers, detectives, and investigators, except that the percentage of the salary of the law enforcement officer paid for by funds from a grant awarded under this section shall not be more than the percentage of the officer’s time on duty that is dedicated to working on cases involving child human trafficking;

children.label: i
children.text: wire taps;

children.label: ii
children.text: consultants with expertise specific to cases involving child human trafficking;

children.label: iii
children.text: travel; and

children.label: iv
children.text: other technical assistance expenditures;

children.label: C
children.text: dedicated anti-trafficking prosecution units, including the funding of salaries for State and local prosecutors, including assisting in paying trial expenses for prosecution of child human trafficking offenders, except that the percentage of the total salary of a State or local prosecutor that is paid using an award under this section shall be not more than the percentage of the total number of hours worked by the prosecutor that is spent working on cases involving child human trafficking;

children.label: i
children.text: child advocacy centers;

children.label: ii
children.text: social service agencies;

children.label: iii
children.text: State governmental health service agencies;

children.label: iv
children.text: housing agencies;

children.label: v
children.text: legal services agencies; and

children.label: vi
children.text: nongovernmental organizations and shelter service providers with substantial experience in delivering wrap-around services to victims of child human trafficking; and

children.label: E
children.text: the establishment or enhancement of other necessary victim assistance programs or personnel, such as victim or child advocates, child-protective services, child forensic interviews, or other necessary service providers;

activities of law enforcement agencies to find homeless and runaway youth, including salaries and associated expenses for retired Federal law enforcement officers assisting the law enforcement agencies in finding homeless and runaway youth; and

children.label: A
children.text: mandatory and regular training requirements for judicial officials involved in the administration or operation of the court program described under this paragraph;

children.label: B
children.text: continuing judicial supervision of victims of child human trafficking, including case worker or child welfare supervision in collaboration with judicial officers, who have been identified by a law enforcement or judicial officer as a potential victim of child human trafficking, regardless of whether the victim has been charged with a crime related to human trafficking;

children.label: i
children.text: State-administered outpatient treatment;

children.label: ii
children.text: life skills training;

children.label: iii
children.text: housing placement;

children.label: iv
children.text: vocational training;

children.label: v
children.text: education;

children.label: vi
children.text: family support services; and

children.label: vii
children.text: job placement;

children.label: D
children.text: centralized case management involving the consolidation of all of each child human trafficking victim’s cases and offenses, and the coordination of all trafficking victim treatment programs and social services;

children.label: E
children.text: regular and mandatory court appearances by the victim during the duration of the treatment program for purposes of ensuring compliance and effectiveness;

children.label: F
children.text: the ultimate dismissal of relevant non-violent criminal charges against the victim, where such victim successfully complies with the terms of the court-ordered treatment program; and

children.label: G
children.text: collaborative efforts with child advocacy centers, child welfare agencies, shelters, and nongovernmental organizations with substantial experience in delivering wrap-around services to victims of child human trafficking to provide services to victims and encourage cooperation with law enforcement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible entity shall submit an application to the Attorney General for a grant under this section in such form and manner as the Attorney General may require.

children.label: A
children.text: describe the activities for which assistance under this section is sought;

children.label: B
children.text: include a detailed plan for the use of funds awarded under the grant;

children.label: C
children.text: provide such additional information and assurances as the Attorney General determines to be necessary to ensure compliance with the requirements of this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: any other grant funding from the Department of Justice or from any other Federal department or agency for purposes similar to those described in subsection (b) for which the eligible entity has applied, and which application is pending on the date of the submission of an application under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other such grant funding that the eligible entity has received during the 5-year period ending on the date of the submission of an application under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the application includes a plan to use awarded funds to engage in all activities described under paragraphs (1) through (3) of subsection (b); or

children.label: B
children.text: the application includes a plan by the State or unit of local government to continue funding of all activities funded by the award after the expiration of the award.

children.heading: Eligible entities soliciting data on child human trafficking
children.label: 4 Eligible entities soliciting data on child human trafficking
children.text: No eligible entity shall be disadvantaged in being awarded a grant under subsection (a) on the grounds that the eligible entity has only recently begun soliciting data on child human trafficking.

children.heading: Duration and renewal of award
children.label: d Duration and renewal of award

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A grant under this section shall expire 3 years after the date of award of the grant.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 2 Renewal
children.text: A grant under this section shall be renewable not more than 2 times and for a period of not greater than 2 years.

enter into a contract with a nongovernmental organization, including an academic or nonprofit organization, that has experience with issues related to child human trafficking and evaluation of grant programs to conduct periodic evaluations of grants made under this section to determine the impact and effectiveness of programs funded with grants awarded under this section;
instruct the Inspector General of the Department of Justice to review evaluations issued under paragraph (1) to determine the methodological and statistical validity of the evaluations; and

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Mandatory exclusion
children.label: f Mandatory exclusion
children.text: An eligible entity awarded funds under this section that is found to have used grant funds for any unauthorized expenditure or otherwise unallowable cost shall not be eligible for any grant funds awarded under the block grant for 2 fiscal years following the year in which the unauthorized expenditure or unallowable cost is reported.

children.heading: Compliance requirement
children.label: g Compliance requirement
children.text: An eligible entity shall not be eligible to receive a grant under this section if within the 5 fiscal years before submitting an application for a grant under this section, the grantee has been found to have violated the terms or conditions of a Government grant program by utilizing grant funds for unauthorized expenditures or otherwise unallowable costs.

children.heading: Administrative cap
children.label: h Administrative cap
children.text: The cost of administering the grants authorized by this section shall not exceed 5 percent of the total amount expended to carry out this section.
70 percent in the first year;
60 percent in the second year; and
50 percent in the third year, and in all subsequent years.

children.heading: Authorization of funding; fully offset
children.label: j Authorization of funding; fully offset
children.text: For purposes of carrying out this section, the Attorney General, in consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, is authorized to award not more than $7,000,000 of the funds available in the Domestic Trafficking Victims’ Fund, established under , for each of fiscal years 2016 through 2020.section 3014 of title 18
the term “child” means a person under the age of 18;
the term “child advocacy center” means a center created under subtitle A of the Victims of Child Abuse Act of 1990 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 130011See References in Text note below.1
the term “child human trafficking” means 1 or more severe forms of trafficking in persons (as defined in ) involving a victim who is a child; andsection 7102 of title 22

children.label: A
children.text: has significant criminal activity involving child human trafficking;

children.label: B
children.text: has demonstrated cooperation between Federal, State, local, and, where applicable, tribal law enforcement agencies, prosecutors, and social service providers in addressing child human trafficking;

children.label: i
children.text: the establishment of a shelter for victims of child human trafficking, through existing or new facilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: the provision of trauma-informed, gender-responsive rehabilitative care to victims of child human trafficking;

children.label: iii
children.text: the provision of specialized training for law enforcement officers and social service providers for all forms of human trafficking, with a focus on domestic child human trafficking;

children.label: iv
children.text: prevention, deterrence, and prosecution of offenses involving child human trafficking, including soliciting, patronizing, or purchasing human acts with children;

children.label: v
children.text: cooperation or referral agreements with organizations providing outreach or other related services to runaway and homeless youth;

children.label: vi
children.text: law enforcement protocols or procedures to screen all individuals arrested for prostitution, whether adult or child, for victimization by sex trafficking and by other crimes, such as sexual assault and domestic violence; and

children.label: vii
children.text: cooperation or referral agreements with State child welfare agencies and child advocacy centers; and

children.label: D
children.text: provides an assurance that, under the plan under subparagraph (C), a victim of child human trafficking shall not be required to collaborate with law enforcement officers to have access to any shelter or services provided with a grant under this section.

children.heading: Grant accountability; specialized victims’ service requirement
children.label: l Grant accountability; specialized victims’ service requirement
children.text: No grant funds under this section may be awarded or transferred to any entity unless such entity has demonstrated substantial experience providing services to victims of human trafficking or related populations (such as runaway and homeless youth), or employs staff specialized in the treatment of human trafficking victims.
